visa_types.overview:
Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings. 

Jordanian and Iraqi citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Jordan or Iraq the last 6 months, can apply for a family immigration permit in Norway at the Visa Application Centre.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) you will find additional information about different family immigration permits and how to apply.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family immigration is also called family reunification or forming a family. Those who apply for family immigration are usually the spouse, cohabitant or child of someone who lives in Norway. Other types of family members who may apply are parents who have children in Norway, those who are going to marry someone in Norway (fiancées/fiancés), foster children and full siblings. 

Jordanian and Iraqi citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Jordan or Iraq the last 6 months, can apply for a family immigration permit in Norway at the Visa Application Centre.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) you will find additional information about different family immigration permits and how to apply.

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid online to confirm the appointment slot or in cash at the Visa Application Centre for walk-ins and is non-refundable.
The Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[ nor > jor > en > Study, Work And Other Permits > table]
- Courier re-submission:
- “The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.

## Documents Required
You must gather all the documents in the relevant checklist on UDI’s website (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
Please check this list (https://www.norway.no/contentassets/d2cbd23c2fef4a609bb98210cb52acc5/personal-documents-in-arabic-english-and-norwegian-20210805.pdf) for information on what documents on the checklist that must be legalized and/or submitted in original. The list also has the names of these documents in Arabic.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
The waiting time at (UDI) the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration Click Here (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/)

## Download Forms
- The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online. To register an application online, please log on to the Application Portal Norway (https://my.udi.no/)
.
- For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
.
 Form for Granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)

visa_types.forms: Checklist for Study, Cultural and Sport Events for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of travel is to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland to attend courses, internships, participation in study/exchange programs/summer schools; participate in sports, training, cultural exchange programs, and participation in conferences, workshops, etc.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of travel is to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland to attend courses, internships, participation in study/exchange programs/summer schools; participate in sports, training, cultural exchange programs, and participation in conferences, workshops, etc.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
 The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre and is non-refundable.
                                                                      
The Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[ nor > jor > en >Family Member Of EU/EEA National ]
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.

## Documents Required
 Checklist for Study, Cultural and Sport Events for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy has received the application, usually the following working day, after the application is submitted at VFS. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time in these cases may be up to 45 calendar days. For more information about the processing time, kindly check: Waiting time - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)

## Download Forms
Checklist for Study, Cultural and Sport Events for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland

visa_types.name: Residence Permits in Norway
visa_types.overview: If you intend to stay in Norway for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Norway, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.
visa_types.full_text: If you intend to stay in Norway for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Norway, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.

Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Residence Cards for Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/residence-card-for-family-members-of-eueea-nationals-/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are from a country outside the EU/EEA and you want to join a family member in Norway who is an EU/EEA national, you can apply for a residence card for family members of EU/EEA nationals.

Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Residence Cards for Family Members of EU/EEA Nationals on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/residence-card-for-family-members-of-eueea-nationals-/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid online to confirm the appointment slot or in cash at the Visa Application Centre for walk-ins and is non-refundable.
Residence Card for Family Member of EU/EEA national has no application fee. You have to fill in the form Application for a permit for residence or work and please contact the Norwegian embassy in Amman by email first (visa.amman@mfa.no (mailto:visa.amman@mfa.no)), in order to get information about how to proceed.

## Documents Required
For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
For processing times, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/#link-2318).

## Download Forms
For the application form for a residence permit in Norway click here
For checklists in family immigration cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
.Form for Granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.

If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to 20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays. 

Jordanian and Iraqi citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Jordan or Iraq the last 6 months, can apply for a residence permit in Norway at the Visa Application Centre.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) you will find additional information about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.

If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to 20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays. 

Jordanian and Iraqi citizens, and persons who have been legally residing in Jordan or Iraq the last 6 months, can apply for a residence permit in Norway at the Visa Application Centre.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information. On the websites of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) you will find additional information about different residence permits for work, study and other purposes.

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid online to confirm the appointment slot or in cash at the Visa Application Centre for walk-ins and is non-refundable.
The Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[ nor > jor > en > Study, Work And Other Permits > table]
- Courier re-submission:
- “The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.

## Documents Required
- For checklists in residence permit cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
The waiting time at (UDI) the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration Click Here (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/case-processing-times/)

## Download Forms
- The application form for a residence permit in Norway needs to be filled online. To register an application online, please log on to the Application Portal Norway (https://my.udi.no/)
.
- For checklists in residence permit cases, please visit the website of the Norwegian Directorate for Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explain-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application/)
Form for Granting Power of Attorney (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/power-of-attorney-e.pdf)

visa_types.name: Visitor's Visas
visa_types.overview: If you plan to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland for less than 90 days, you can apply for a visitor's visa. (Schengen– type C).
visa_types.full_text: If you plan to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland for less than 90 days, you can apply for a visitor's visa. (Schengen– type C).

visa_types.name: Residence Permits in Denmark
visa_types.overview: If you intend to stay in Denmark for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Denmark, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.
visa_types.full_text: If you intend to stay in Denmark for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Denmark, you need to apply for a Residence Permit. Please contact Embassy (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/) for information.

visa_types.name: Visiting family and friends
visa_types.documents_required:
- Checklist. Visiting Family and Friends visa for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland.
General Information
All documents must be A4 with no staples, no stickers and no torn pages.
In some cases, further documents and guarantees may be required. 
Please Note:
All applicants must apply in person.
Some documents in Arabic must be translated into English- include original and a copy of the original.

Notes:
- You cannot work while you are visiting Norway/Schengen on a visitor’s visa.
- You cannot get a visitor’s visa to apply for a residence permit in Norway/Schengen area.
- A visa is only intended for short-term stays in the Schengen region and not for permanent or long-term residency.
- If you attempt to use a visa stay in Norway or another Schengen country to obtain permanent or long-term residency you can be given a penalty period. This means that you would not normally be able to obtain a visa to enter Schengen countries during that period.
visa_types.visa_fees:
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
 The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre and is non-refundable.
                                                                      
The Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[ nor > jor > en >Family Member Of EU/EEA National ]
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.
visa_types.processing_time: The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy has received the application, usually the following working day, after the application is submitted at VFS. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time in these cases may be up to 45 calendar days. For more information about the processing time, kindly check: Waiting time - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)
visa_types.forms:
Checklist. Visiting Family and Friends visa for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland.

Sponsorship Form: If you are visiting family or friends, the person you will be visiting will normally have to provide a Sponsorship. For more information about Guarantee for applicants visiting on a visitor's visa, please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/guarantee-for-applicants-visiting-on-a-visitors-visa-/).
Power of attorney: This form is submitted together with the application if the applicant wishes to give another person authorization to follow up on the case on his/her behalf. For more information about Power of attorney, please click here (http://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/power-of-attorney--authorisation-/).
To download the Application Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland as a tourist, by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets and accommodation.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

## Overview

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

## Visa Fees

## Photo Specifications

## Processing Time

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of travel is to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland for business/commercial activity/ meetings/conferences/fairs.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of travel is to visit Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland for business/commercial activity/ meetings/conferences/fairs.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
 The Service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid in cash at the Visa Application Centre and is non-refundable.
                                                                      
The Visa Application Centre offers an optional service at a nominal additional cost:

[ nor > jor > en >Family Member Of EU/EEA National ]
Courier re-submission:
“The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. The applicant may later resubmit the passport for issuance of visa. There is no additional fee for this service.

## Documents Required
- Checklist. Business Visa for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days after the embassy has received the application, usually the following working day, after the application is submitted at VFS. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time in these cases may be up to 45 calendar days. For more information about the processing time, kindly check: Waiting time - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/waitingtime/#link-2319)

## Download Forms
Checklist. Business Visa for Norway, Denmark, Iceland and Finland.
VU1_Form (Denmark)

visa_types.name: Family Immigration and other permits to Denmark
visa_types.documents_required: - Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for document requirements for different residence permits in Denmark.
visa_types.visa_fees:
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
For application fee please note:
- Go online to New to Denmark (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/) and register your application. Pay the required fee to obtain an ID number.
- Print out the application and the checklist showing the required documents.
- Collect all the documents listed in the checklist.
- Register your (family immigration/work permit/study permit) application again at Log in - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/my-page/) to pay the fees in the Norwegian system, and print out the receipt only.
- Visit the VFS Global application center in Amman (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor) to submit your application.
- You will be requested to pay a service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid online to confirm the appointment slot or in cash at the Visa Application Centre for walk-ins and is non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time: Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for processing times.
visa_types.forms: - Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for document requirements for different residence permits in Denmark.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you intend to stay in Denmark for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Denmark, you need to apply for Residence Permit.

For an overview of the different residence permits in Denmark, please visit the web page New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm). The web page will also provide you with a list showing which documents you need to hand in with your application.

Please be aware that you have to pay a new application fee when your submit your application at the Norway Visa Application Centre.

Go to each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you intend to stay in Denmark for more than 90 days, or if you are going to work while in Denmark, you need to apply for Residence Permit.

For an overview of the different residence permits in Denmark, please visit the web page New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm). The web page will also provide you with a list showing which documents you need to hand in with your application.

Please be aware that you have to pay a new application fee when your submit your application at the Norway Visa Application Centre.

Go to each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

## Visa Fees
For Visa fee list click here (https://udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/)
For application fee please note:
- Go online to New to Denmark (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/) and register your application. Pay the required fee to obtain an ID number.
- Print out the application and the checklist showing the required documents.
- Collect all the documents listed in the checklist.
- Register your (family immigration/work permit/study permit) application again at Log in - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/my-page/) to pay the fees in the Norwegian system, and print out the receipt only.
- Visit the VFS Global application center in Amman (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor) to submit your application.
- You will be requested to pay a service fee of 20 Euro (equivalent to JOD) is paid online to confirm the appointment slot or in cash at the Visa Application Centre for walk-ins and is non-refundable.

## Documents Required
- Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for document requirements for different residence permits in Denmark.

## Photo Specifications
Please click here  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/photopassport-photo/)for the requirements for the photo that you must hand in with your application.

## Processing Time
Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for processing times.

## Download Forms
- Go to New to Denmark – the Official Portal for Foreigners  (https://www.nyidanmark.dk/en-us/frontpage.htm) for document requirements for different residence permits in Denmark.

Checklist for family member of EU/EEA national
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the travel is to visit an EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland. Only spouses and children can apply, as well as parents who are dependent ascendants of the EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway or one of the represented countries.

A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.

Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The purpose of the travel is to visit an EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland. Only spouses and children can apply, as well as parents who are dependent ascendants of the EU/EEA citizen exercising free movement rights in Norway or one of the represented countries.

A Schengen visa only allows foreign nationals to visit Norway and the other Schengen countries for up to 90 days.

Please click on each of the tabs above for more information. You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/) and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The purpose of the visit is to travel to Norway, Denmark, Finland or Iceland as a tourist, by taking a trip organised by a tour operator, or through individual booking of tickets and accommodation.

Please click on each of the tabs above and How to Apply (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/jor/en/nor/apply-visa) for more information.

You can also find additional information about Visitor’s visa on the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/)and the Norwegian Embassy in Amman (https://www.norway.no/en/jordan/).
